_source: National Electrical Code 2023, Mike Holt's Illustrated Guide, NECA standards
_note: Long-tail electrical code data. Specific ampacity, conduit fill, grounding rules electricians must know.

derating_factors.temperature_correction: Above 86°F ambient, derate per NEC Table 310.15(B)(1)
derating_factors.continuous_load: Load on circuit for 3+ hours; size wire for 125% of continuous load

voltage_drop_rule.nec_recommendation: Max 3% drop on branch circuits; 5% total feeders + branch
voltage_drop_rule.formula: VD = (2 × K × I × L) / CM where K=12.9 (copper), I=current, L=one-way length(ft), CM=circular mils
voltage_drop_rule.practical: For 120V 20A circuit 100ft run: 12 AWG has 2.4% drop (acceptable); 14 AWG has 3.8% (upgrade to 12)

box_fill_calculations.nec_314_16: Every wire, device, clamp, fitting counts toward box fill capacity

counting_rules.each_conductor: Count each unbroken conductor entering box
counting_rules.cable_clamps: Count one conductor size for one or more clamps
counting_rules.device_yoke: Count two conductor sizes for each device (switch, receptacle)
counting_rules.equipment_ground: Count one conductor for all grounds combined
counting_rules.pigtails: Do not count; only count conductors originating outside box

example_calculation.scenario: Standard single-gang box with one switch, 12 AWG wire, 2 cables entering
example_calculation.count: 2 hot (in/out) + 2 neutral + 1 ground (all combined) + 1 clamp + 2 switch = 8 conductors
example_calculation.box_options: Single-gang deep box (20.3 cu in) acceptable; standard (14 cu in) NOT acceptable

grounding_vs_bonding.grounding: Intentional connection to earth; provides fault current path and voltage stabilization
grounding_vs_bonding.bonding: Connection between metal parts to ensure electrical continuity and equal potential

equipment_grounding_conductor_sizing.rule: NEC Table 250.122; size based on overcurrent device, not wire size

grounding_electrode_system.grounding_electrode_conductor: Service size ≤100A: 8 AWG copper minimum; 101-200A: 6 AWG; >200A: consult Table 250.66

gfci_and_afci_requirements.afci_required: All 120V 15A and 20A branch circuits in dwelling units (bedrooms, living, dining, etc) NEC 210.12
gfci_and_afci_requirements.afci_function: Detects arc faults (damaged wires, loose connections); prevents fires

kitchen_requirements.small_appliance_circuits: Minimum 2 dedicated 20A circuits for countertop receptacles (NEC 210.11)
kitchen_requirements.refrigerator: Dedicated 15A or 20A circuit recommended (not required by code but best practice)
kitchen_requirements.disposal: Can share with dishwasher if on 20A circuit

bathroom_requirements.receptacle_circuit: Minimum one 20A circuit for bathroom receptacles (can serve multiple bathrooms if only for receptacles)
bathroom_requirements.cannot_share_with: Bathroom receptacle circuit cannot also serve lights or exhaust fan per NEC 210.11(C)(3)

bedroom_requirements.afci_protection: Required on all bedroom circuits since NEC 2002
bedroom_requirements.receptacle_spacing: Max 12ft between receptacles; max 6ft from doorway
bedroom_requirements.switched_lighting: Every habitable room must have wall-switch controlled lighting

conduit_types.emt: Electrical Metallic Tubing; most common indoor; lightweight; compression or set-screw fittings
conduit_types.pvc_schedule_80: Above-ground outdoor; thicker wall than Schedule 40
conduit_types.flexible_metal: FMC; short runs to motors, fixtures; not suitable as equipment ground

conduit_fill_table.example: 3/4" EMT with 3+ conductors: max 0.213 sq in; fits five 12 AWG THHN (0.0133 sq in each = 0.0665 total)

bending_rules.reason: Beyond 360°, cannot pull wire without damaging insulation
bending_rules.radius_minimums: EMT 3/4": 4" radius; 1": 5.25"; larger conduit requires larger sweep

service_size_determination.general_lighting: 3 VA per sq ft dwelling; 2 VA commercial
service_size_determination.small_appliance: 1500 VA per circuit × 2 minimum in kitchen
service_size_determination.appliances: List nameplate ratings; apply demand factors per NEC 220.53

demand_factors.ac_cooling: Larger of heat or AC (not both)

example_200A_service.2000_sq_ft_house: General lighting: 6000 VA; small appliance: 3000 VA; laundry: 1500 VA; range: 8000 VA; dryer: 5000 VA; AC: 6000 VA; heat: 15000 VA

common_violations.double_tapped_breaker: Two wires under one terminal screw; violation unless breaker rated for it (most aren't); causes arcing
common_violations.backstabbed_receptacles: Push-in connections; legal but unreliable; leads to loose connections and heat
common_violations.missing_gfci: Bathroom, kitchen, outdoor without GFCI; immediate safety hazard
common_violations.open_junction_box: All splices must be in accessible, covered box; no buried splices in walls
common_violations.wrong_breaker_size: 20A breaker on 14 AWG wire; fire hazard; wire must match breaker
common_violations.unprotected_nm_cable: Romex below 8ft needs conduit or guard strips; physical damage protection
common_violations.improper_stapling: NM cable must be stapled within 12" of box and every 4.5ft along run
